title: Vancouver Geography - Location, Terrain & Climate
content:
Vancouver is a major city located in the province of British Columbia, Canada, situated on the western coast of North America, surrounded by water on three sides and nestled in the Coast Mountains.

[FACTBOX]
Land Area: 114 km², about the size of Brooklyn
Highest Point: Mount Seymour, 1,451 meters
Lowest Point: Sea level, 0 meters
Coastline: 18 km, along the English Bay and Burrard Inlet
Borders: Burnaby, Richmond, Surrey, and North Vancouver
Climate Type: **Temperate**
[/FACTBOX]

[SECTION] Location & Borders [/SECTION]
Vancouver is situated on the Burrard Peninsula, surrounded by water on three sides, with the Coast Mountains to the north and east, and the Fraser River to the south. The city is bordered by the municipalities of Burnaby, Richmond, Surrey, and North Vancouver, and is part of the larger Metro Vancouver region. Vancouver is also close to the US-Canada border, with the city of Seattle, Washington, located just a few hours' drive to the south.

The city's unique location, with its proximity to the ocean and mountains, makes it a popular destination for outdoor enthusiasts, with easy access to skiing, hiking, and water sports. Vancouver is also a major transportation hub, with a busy international airport, a large seaport, and a comprehensive public transportation system. The city's location on the Pacific coast also makes it an important center for trade and commerce, with strong connections to Asia and other parts of the world.

[SECTION] Terrain & Landscape [/SECTION]
Vancouver's terrain is characterized by its proximity to the Coast Mountains, with several large mountains, including Mount Seymour, Grouse Mountain, and Cypress Mountain, located just north of the city. The city itself is situated on a peninsula, with the Burrard Inlet to the north and the English Bay to the west. The terrain is generally hilly, with several smaller mountains and ridges, including Queen Elizabeth Park, which offers stunning views of the city and the surrounding mountains.

The city's landscape is also dominated by its many parks and green spaces, including Stanley Park, which is one of the largest urban parks in North America. The park features several beaches, hiking trails, and a large forest, and is a popular destination for both locals and tourists. Vancouver's terrain and landscape have also been shaped by its history of settlement and development, with many of the city's neighborhoods and communities located on flat, low-lying areas near the water.

The city's unique terrain and landscape have also made it a popular destination for outdoor enthusiasts, with many opportunities for hiking, skiing, and water sports. The nearby mountains offer several world-class ski resorts, including Whistler Blackcomb, which is located just a few hours' drive north of the city. The city's many parks and green spaces also offer a range of outdoor activities, including walking, cycling, and kayaking.

[SECTION] Climate By Season [/SECTION]
[TABLE]
| Season | Months | Temperature Range | Rainfall | Conditions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Spring | March to May | 10-18°C | 80-100 mm | Mild, rainy |
| Summer | June to August | 18-25°C | 40-60 mm | Warm, dry |
| Autumn | September to November | 10-18°C | 100-120 mm | Cool, rainy |
| Winter | December to February | 3-8°C | 60-80 mm | Cool, wet |
[/TABLE]

[SECTION] Best Time To Visit [/SECTION]
[CALLOUT] Best months: September to November and March to May. Why: These periods offer mild temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal for outdoor activities and sightseeing. Avoid: December to February, as the weather can be cool and wet, and June to August, as the city can be crowded with tourists. [/CALLOUT]

[SECTION] Natural Highlights [/SECTION]
- Stanley Park: a large urban park featuring several beaches, hiking trails, and a large forest
- Grouse Mountain: a popular ski resort and hiking destination located just north of the city
- Capilano Suspension Bridge Park: a popular tourist attraction featuring suspension bridges, hiking trails, and a forest canopy walk
- Lynn Canyon Park: a scenic park featuring hiking trails, waterfalls, and a suspension bridge
- Cypress Mountain: a popular ski resort and hiking destination located just north of the city
- Queen Elizabeth Park: a scenic park featuring gardens, walking trails, and stunning views of the city and the surrounding mountains
